title: clinopodium albanicum
intro: Clinopodium albanicum, also known as Albanian calamintha or Albanian wild basil, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its expectorant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including relieving respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as soothing digestive problems. Clinopodium albanicum is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its essential oil is used in aromatherapy and herbal remedies to promote overall well-being.
